claim: Independent verification confirms YOLO demand evidence dispatch system enforces scope separation: project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths; external research must be marked scope=external; boundary audit prevents project mutation.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules: every evidence record must declare scope (project/external/user/unknown); project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative path; external research from WebFetch/WebSearch must be marked scope=external.
evidence.why: Core agent protocol that enforces scope separation at the point of evidence generation.

evidence.summary: Required JSON schema for evidence records defines path (repo-relative for project scope), url (for external scope), line, scope, source, summary, and why fields.
evidence.why: Formalizes the evidence record structure with separate fields for project paths vs external URLs.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies evidence scope: checks declared scope, infers from source type (external_web vs project_code), detects URL patterns, defaults to project if path exists.
evidence.why: Scope classification logic that prevents external web evidence from being treated as project facts.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates blockers when: (1) evidence marked project-scoped lacks repo-relative path, or (2) evidence is only external/user/unknown for existing-project facts.
evidence.why: Enforcement mechanism blocking PRD readiness when project facts lack proper project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: Validation helpers check evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope (VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES) and evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator (path field exists, not URL).
evidence.why: Supporting validation functions used by scope checking and PRD readiness logic.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors validates all evidence records declare scope as one of: project, external, user, or unknown.
evidence.why: Schema validation that enforces scope declaration before evidence acceptance.

evidence.summary: PRD readiness check loops through agent results and applies both evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues to generate blockers.
evidence.why: Integration point where evidence scope validation blocks PRD readiness if requirements not met.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: evidence records must declare scope; project evidence requires repo-relative path; external evidence can only be background research, not proof of existing project fields/APIs/states.
evidence.why: Product specification confirming intent and boundaries of evidence scope system.

evidence.summary: Runtime execution builds boundary snapshot before/after agent runs, detects any project file mutations outside allowed artifact root, and generates boundary blockers.
evidence.why: Boundary audit mechanism ensuring evidence agents cannot modify target project files.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION documents required evidence record structure and scope semantics.
evidence.why: Schema definition formalizing evidence result contract with scope requirements.

risks: Evidence agent implementation may have edge cases where ambiguous evidence gets misclassified despite the documented rules.

result.notes: Independent verification confirms the explorer's findings. The evidence dispatch system has comprehensive scope separation built into the agent protocol, schema, validation logic, scope classification, PRD readiness checks, and runtime boundary auditing. The system explicitly allows WebFetch/WebSearch for external research marked as scope=external while requiring project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths for target-project facts.
